title: Shanghai 2-Day City Break
metaTitle: Shanghai 2-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Shanghai, China: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Shanghai Tower observation deck and Yu Garden. Prices from $1406 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Shanghai, China — Humid subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 32°C) and cold, damp winters (highs around 8°C, occasional light snow). March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures avoiding summer humidity and winter cold. This itinerary blends Shanghai Tower observation deck, Yu Garden, The Bund with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Shanghai Tower observation deck, Yu Garden, The Bund
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Shanghai
itinerary.description: Land at Shanghai Pudong International Airport (PVG) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Shanghai Tower observation deck — One of the world's tallest buildings, with the world's fastest elevators. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Shanghai Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Yu Garden — Classical Ming-dynasty garden in the old city. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to PVG for your onward flight.

travelTips: Set up a VPN before you arrive — Google, WhatsApp, and Instagram are blocked in mainland China.
Check whether you qualify for the 144-hour visa-free transit if Shanghai is a stopover on a longer trip.
Take the Maglev train from Pudong Airport at least once for the novelty of 430km/h ground travel.
Have your hotel's address written in Chinese characters for taxi drivers.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A VPN set up before arrival for accessing blocked apps and sites
A backup of cash alongside digital payment apps
thingsToDo: Bike tour of the French Concession
thingsToAvoid: Arriving without a VPN arranged — it's very difficult to set one up once inside mainland China's firewall.
Relying solely on cash — many local shops and taxis increasingly expect WeChat Pay or Alipay.
Assuming English will be widely spoken outside major hotels and tourist sites.
topRestaurants: Ultraviolet by Paul Pairet — Avant-garde tasting menu ($$$$)
instagramSpots: The Bund facing Pudong's skyline at night
hiddenGems: Former Jewish Refugees Museum in Tilanqiao
adventureActivities: Bike tour of the French Concession
luxuryExperiences: Tasting menu at Ultraviolet by Paul Pairet
Suite stay with a Bund/Pudong skyline view
honeymoonExperiences: The Bund at night, skyline lit up
Dinner in a French Concession courtyard restaurant
localEtiquette: A firm handshake and business-card exchange with both hands is standard in professional settings.
Major Western apps (Google, WhatsApp, Instagram) are blocked — a VPN arranged before arrival is essential for connectivity.
Most payments are cashless via WeChat Pay or Alipay, which can be tricky to set up as a short-term visitor — carry some cash as backup.
localTransportGuide: The Shanghai Metro is extensive, cheap, and easy to navigate with English signage; the Maglev train covers the airport-to-city run at up to 430km/h. Rideshare apps (Didi) work well, though a China-registered payment method or cash can simplify things for short-term visitors.
bestTimeToVisit: March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures avoiding summer humidity and winter cold.
weather: Humid subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 32°C) and cold, damp winters (highs around 8°C, occasional light snow).
visaInformation: Most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India need a Chinese visa arranged in advance, though many travelers qualify for a 144-hour visa-free transit if arriving/departing via certain international gateways including Shanghai.
averageFlightTime: ~10.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Shanghai?
faqs.answer: Most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India need a Chinese visa arranged in advance, though many travelers qualify for a 144-hour visa-free transit if arriving/departing via certain international gateways including Shanghai.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Shanghai?
faqs.answer: March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures avoiding summer humidity and winter cold. Humid subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 32°C) and cold, damp winters (highs around 8°C, occasional light snow).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Shanghai safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Shanghai is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Shanghai?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CNY. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Mia Wang
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Yu Garden and French Concession. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Olivia Kowalski
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Our trip to Shanghai was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to French Concession was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Mohammed Cohen
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: The Bund alone was worth the trip, but combined with Shanghai Tower observation deck this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ava Larsen
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Nanjing Road alone was worth the trip, but combined with Shanghai Tower observation deck this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: David Patel
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Shanghai is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Zhujiajiao water town day trip. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Arjun Botha
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Tianzifang in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Noah Müller
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Shanghai was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Tianzifang was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Yu Garden in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Yu Garden and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
